reasoning: Q1 is already elite-performing (62 ms, 1.8x faster than DuckDB) with no correctness issues. We have 1 iteration remaining and high confidence from iteration 1's improvement. Prioritize two high-ROI, low-risk optimizations: Zone Map Skipping (5-10 ms I/O reduction) and Compact Hash Tables (1-2 ms hash lookup speedup). Combined gain of 6-12 ms (10-20% improvement) targets reaching ~50-55 ms, solidifying our competitive lead.
focus_areas: Zone Map Skipping on l_shipdate: load min/max metadata from zone_map.idx, skip blocks where min(l_shipdate) > CUTOFF_DATE (1998-09-02), implement skip vector in morsel assignment
Replace std::unordered_map with Compact Hash Table (Abseil::flat_hash_map or Robin Hood): improve cache locality and reduce hash operation overhead from 5-10% to ~2-3% of execution time
strategy_notes: Both optimizations are straightforward, have proven implementation patterns (available in knowledge base), and low regression risk. Zone Map Skipping targets I/O reduction; Compact Hash Tables target CPU efficiency. Combined effort should yield 6-12 ms wall-clock improvement without requiring loop restructuring (unlike SIMD filtering). Avoid Priority 5 (Kahan simplification) due to precision risk—not worth the complexity for 1-2 ms gain.
notes: This is our final optimization opportunity. Selecting these two allows us to push Q1 further ahead of DuckDB while maintaining correctness. If successful, iteration 3 should focus on validation and profiling rather than additional changes. The 1.8x advantage over DuckDB is already strong; these tweaks extend it to 2x+.
